orgPolicy.title: Compensation Bands Now in Workday
orgPolicy.summary: Comp band GSheets are no longer functional. PeopleTech has built a live Workday report as the permanent replacement.
bullets: Run the report at https://wd5.myworkday.com/gusto/d/inst/1422$5089/rel-task/3001$14.htmld — bookmark it
Search by Job Family; returns all ranges across levels and comp zones
Workday is now the source of truth for comp bands, bonus targets, and more
PEs have a master comp spreadsheet as interim — do not share broadly; ping your PE for comp info
Questions? Submit a ticket at go/phc

tp.point: Comp band GSheets are gone — use the new Workday report. Bookmark the link. Ping your PE if you need comp info now.

orgPolicy.title: FY27 Offer Calculators Updated
orgPolicy.summary: Updated offer calculators for L1–L6 reflecting new variable comp targets are now live.
bullets: L6 variable target: now 25% (up from 20%)
L1–L5 Hourly: $1,250 flat variable target
Calculators saved in the Invite Drive folder — use for all offers going forward

tp.point: Use the updated FY27 offer calculators for all new offers. L6 variable is now 25%.

orgPolicy.title: Scorecards Must Include a Human-Written Summary
orgPolicy.summary: Consistent pattern of HC pushback on AI-only transcripts. Every scorecard needs a human-authored summary — no exceptions.
bullets: Raw BrightHire/AI transcripts without a written summary are NOT sufficient
Every interviewer must write their own synthesis and take — AI assists, doesn't replace
Scorecard-only transcripts are flagged in HC and reflect on Invite as a function
Do NOT submit to HC if a scorecard is missing its summary — a delayed submission beats an incomplete one
Hold your interviewers accountable; automations to ease this are in progress

tp.point: No more transcript-only scorecards. Every interviewer must write their own summary. Don't submit to HC until it's done.

orgPolicy.title: Cycle 2 Now Live: June 15 – July 31
orgPolicy.summary: Cycle 2 kicked off today. Initiative pages need 'What is this?' and 'Done when...' fields completed.
bullets: 6 initiatives already logged in the Invite Cycle Planning Tracker
All initiative pages must have 'What is this?' (2–4 sentences) and 'Done when...' (3–6 checkboxes)
Use the Invite Cycle Planner Claude skill to add/update — no manual Notion wrangling required
Invite Cycle Planning Tracker: https://app.notion.com/p/Invite-Cycle-Planning-Tracker-33c75cefee89443da577aa33f71f5f95

tp.point: Cycle 2 is live. Your PE will be connecting with you on priorities. Mid-quarter check-ins happening next week — no prep needed.

orgPolicy.title: VMA Evolution Launching August 3
orgPolicy.summary: Refreshed VMA program with updated question bank, calibrated rubric, and new AI mindset component. Celena joining PE meeting next week for nomination process walkthrough.
bullets: New AI mindset component assesses adaptability and learning orientation — not technical skills
Nomination flow: I&O partners with Invite PEs; self-volunteering replaced by credentialed process
VMA returning to standalone interview (reverting consolidation with PE/Skills for Eng and CX)
Performance tie-in: VMA participation becomes a formal contribution for L3–L6 Gusties
Escalation path: Kristin Ruehle for L1–L5 HC disagreements; Scott Helmes for L6+
Celena joining PE meeting next week — flag your calendars

tp.point: VMA is being revamped — launching August 3. New nomination process, standalone interview structure, and performance tie-in for L3–L6. More from Celena next week.

orgPolicy.title: Hiring Committee Now Facilitated by Invite Leadership
orgPolicy.summary: Invite Leadership is now running HC sessions and follow-ups. HC questions go to your PE — not Invite Ops.
bullets: For questions or updates on submitted candidates → reach out to your Invite PE directly
Invite Ops still supporting with candidate preps

tp.point: HC questions and updates now go directly to your PE — not Invite Ops.

orgPolicy.title: Variable Cash Award Field Now in Greenhouse Offers
orgPolicy.summary: New offer detail field in Greenhouse: 'Are they receiving a % Variable Cash Award?' Three options: No-Hourly/Not Eligible, Yes-US, Yes-Canada.
bullets: Select Yes-US or Yes-Canada to trigger correct variable comp verbiage in the offer letter
Variable targets: L1–L3 = 5%, L4 = 7.5%, L5 = 10%, L6 = 25%
Program is 50% individual / 50% company performance split
Double-check offer details before sending — accuracy is critical

orgPolicy.title: InviteOS New Features Live
orgPolicy.summary: Several new InviteOS features launched this week. Beta testers also wanted for the Debrief & HC Agent.
bullets: Pending Actions: centralized view of candidate emails, scorecards, and referrals awaiting your action
Submit Feedback button now in-app — sends bugs/ideas directly to PeopleTech Jira
Loom walkthrough: https://www.loom.com/share/aabf4bd1aec243a39d47abba4df6c82b
Beta testers wanted for Debrief & Hiring Committee Agent — reply in #invite-team thread

tp.point: InviteOS has new features — Pending Actions hub and in-app feedback button. Watch the Loom. Beta testers wanted for Debrief & HC Agent.

orgPolicy.title: Process Change Flagging Reminder
orgPolicy.summary: Increase in urgent GH system change requests. Flag process changes early — PeopleTech needs time to test.
bullets: Covers: custom field changes, verbiage updates, any system configuration in Greenhouse
Not sure if it counts? Submit a ticket at go/phc

items.heading: Welcome Party Venue Confirmed: Bierstadt Lagerhaus
bullets: Private bar (3 hrs) + buffet (2 hrs) + exclusive large games area
Time slot: 5–8pm or 5:30–8:30pm — calendar invite coming when agreement signed
Planning 4 teams of 10 (one team of 11)
Tuesday activities still being finalized — reach out to Jaime to suggest or help coordinate
Hackathon pre-work being drafted — input wanted on focus/theme

keyEvents.theme: Greenhouse Open for Ops — Conference Recap

items.heading: Lisa Pham + Alli Belamide attended; key takeaways shared
bullets: Build flexible frameworks, not all-in-one solutions — problem definition is the most important step
Enablement must be ongoing: gamify, hackathon, workshop — one 30-min training isn't enough
Data problem is unstructured, not dirty — audit and align data sources
Time to Fill is a lagging indicator; track conversion/reschedule/feedback rates instead
Quality of Hire belongs to HMs; TA owns Quality of Submittal
Human-in-the-Loop tiers: 100% human (high-judgment), AI-assisted (screening/scheduling), fully automated (status updates)
GH products to watch: Fraud detection, Notetaker, Analytics, Ezra (AI screening)

items.heading: Gusto Closed Friday, June 19 — Juneteenth
bullets: Plan your week accordingly — no work expected June 19

items.heading: Gabe Cadavillo Departing Gusto
bullets: GTM recruiter leaving end of this week after 4+ years
Leaving in great standing — reach out to wish him well

items.heading: Saumya Chopra joining next PE meeting to discuss req approval pain points
bullets: Topics: (1) requisition multi-step approval process and (2) req routing based on job details in Workday
Come prepared with your current friction points on both

items.heading: Mid-quarter check-ins happening next week
bullets: Light performance check-in with your PE — no prep needed on recruiter side
Expect your PE to reach out to schedule
